nodes.label: world | lie | time
nodes.sentence: In the time before memory, when the dense German forests were shrouded in endless night, the world lay in total darkness.

nodes.label: rabe | grow | ∅
nodes.sentence: The Rabe, a cunning black bird who had existed since the beginning of days, grew weary of stumbling blindly through the dark woods and mist.

nodes.label: rabe | discover | cottage
nodes.sentence: One evening, the Rabe discovered the small cottage of a solitary old man who lived deep in the Schwarzwald with his young daughter.

nodes.label: rabe | learn | observation
nodes.sentence: Through quiet observation and craft, the Rabe learned that the old man possessed a priceless treasure: all the light in the world, locked away inside a tiny wooden box, nested within box after box, hidden beneath thick woolen cloths embroidered with ancient runes.

nodes.label: rabe | vow | ∅
nodes.sentence: Determined, the Rabe vowed to bring light back to the shadowed forests.

nodes.label: he | ponder | ∅
nodes.sentence: He pondered long and devised a clever plan.

nodes.label: rabe | transform | himself
nodes.sentence: When the old man’s daughter came to draw water from the cold, dark river, the Rabe transformed himself into a single, sharp spruce needle and drifted gently down the current, slipping into her wooden bucket.

nodes.label: needle | slide | throat
nodes.sentence: As she drank, the needle slid down her throat and settled deep within her warmth.

nodes.label: rabe | change | ∅
nodes.sentence: There, the Rabe changed once more, becoming a small human child.

nodes.label: time | pass | silence
nodes.sentence: Time passed slowly in the dim silence of her womb, until finally the Rabe was born into the world as a peculiar boy.

nodes.label: heart | soften | ∅
nodes.sentence: Though marked by his strange looks, the old man’s heart softened, and he loved this child as a grandson.

nodes.label: he | warn | ∅
nodes.sentence: Yet he fiercely warned that no one should ever touch the sacred box of light.

nodes.label: child | beg | this
nodes.sentence: Despite this, the Rabe child begged to hold the glowing treasure even for a moment.

nodes.label: man | relent | ∅
nodes.sentence: At last, the old man relented.

nodes.label: he | lift | lid
nodes.sentence: He lifted the small box’s lid and revealed a radiant, glowing orb that pulsed like a tiny sun.

nodes.label: he | throw | it
nodes.sentence: He threw it gently toward his grandson.

nodes.label: boy | transform | bird
nodes.sentence: As the glowing orb approached, the boy transformed rapidly into a great black bird, its vast wings unfurling like storm clouds over the forest canopy, its sharp beak wide and ready.

nodes.label: rabe | catch | orb
nodes.sentence: With a triumphant croak, the Rabe caught the orb in his beak.

nodes.label: he | burst | flap
nodes.sentence: With mighty flaps, he burst through the cottage’s thatched roof, flying out through the smoke hole into the night sky, his prize shining like a beacon in his beak.

nodes.label: light | return | day
nodes.sentence: Thus, from that day forward, the light returned to brighten the forests and fields, chasing away the long shadows, and filling the world with day once more.
